nyc_311.csv — data overview

Data Dictionary
Description:The dataset contains **10 000 New York City 311 complaint records** collected between **January 2010 and December 2020**. Each record is uniquely identified by an integer “Unique Key” that ranges from 15,636,252 to 48,471,253 but only 10 000 distinct values are present, indicating a sparse key space. Timestamps capture the full lifecycle of each complaint: when it was created, when it closed (≈2.7 % missing), and the scheduled due date (≈65 % missing). Agency information is provided both as abbreviations and full names, with NYPD, HPD, DOT, DSNY, DEP, and DOB handling the majority of cases. The dataset describes a variety of complaint types—Noise, Heat/Hot Water, Illegal Parking, Street Condition, etc.—and includes free‑text descriptors that often fall into an “Other” bucket, accounting for more than half of all entries. Location is partially geocoded; 25 % of records lack latitude/longitude pairs and over 17 % are missing street address components, yet the majority of coordinates lie within Manhattan’s typical latitudinal (40.11–40.91) and longitudinal (-77.5 to -73.7) ranges.
Rows:10,000
Columns:41
Completeness:68.4%
Compiled:2026-07-15 11:28 UTC
Spatial extent: New Jersey & New York, United States — 9 outliers (Pennsylvania) · North America
